typecheck.command: cd quant-dashboard && python3 -m mypy app/ src/
typecheck.detail: Success: no issues found in 23 source files

lint.detail: 66 errors found (42 fixable). 65 are pre-existing from prior milestones (whitespace, line length in tests and seed scripts). 1 new error introduced by benchmark-visual-overlay: unused pytest import in tests/test_benchmark_comparison.py (F401). Not blocking — prior scrutiny ran ruff only on src/ and app/.

blockingIssues.description: File path mismatch: app/main.py serves benchmark.html from frontend/dist/ (a gitignored build artifact), but the committed source is at frontend/benchmark.html. The dist/ copy is stale (16KB vs 18KB source) and will be deleted on FreqUI rebuild (pnpm run build). Fix: change FileResponse path to serve from frontend/benchmark.html (the source), or add a post-build copy step.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the pattern for serving custom non-FreqUI HTML pages: custom static pages must be defined as explicit FastAPI routes BEFORE the static file mount and SPA fallback. Files should be served from a committed path (e.g., frontend/benchmark.html), not from frontend/dist/ which is gitignored and regenerated by FreqUI builds.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature benchmark-visual-overlay added /benchmark-comparison route serving from frontend/dist/benchmark.html, but the committed source lives at frontend/benchmark.html. The dist/ copy is a stale artifact. This is a deployment-breaking pattern that future custom pages could repeat.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update frequi-compat.md to document: custom static HTML files (like benchmark.html) must NOT be placed in frontend/dist/ — that directory is gitignored and regenerated by pnpm build. Custom pages should be served from committed paths outside dist/ (e.g., frontend/custom-pages/).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: benchmark-visual-overlay feature placed benchmark.html in frontend/ (source) but served it from frontend/dist/ (stale copy). The dist/ copy differs in content and will be lost after a rebuild.

rejectedObservations.observation: services.yaml has no gaps — worker used existing configuration correctly
rejectedObservations.reason: No action needed; confirmed existing config is adequate.
